title: Earthy Jamaican Tandoor Tagine
description: A velvety, macrobiotic-friendly twist on traditional Jamaican cuisine, featuring dry sherry, tandoor-cooked lamb, and aromatic spices.

ingredients.name: tandoor spice blend
ingredients.notes: mix of cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and turmeric

instructions: Preheat the tandoor to 200°C. If you don't have a tandoor, use a conventional oven at 400°F (200°C).
Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and tandoor spice blend, and rub the lamb fat or oil all over the meat.
Brown the lamb shoulder in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat, about 5 minutes per side. Set the lamb aside.
Soften the onions, ginger, and garlic in the same pot over medium heat, about 5 minutes.
Add the red bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the cherry tomatoes, dry sherry (if using), coconut milk, and vegetable broth to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Return the lamb shoulder to the pot, cover, and transfer to the preheated tandoor or conventional oven.
Braise the lamb and vegetables for 2-1/2 to 3 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve over rice or with naan.
